$comment: Funkii AI catalog (Store v2 Phase 2, SPEC_STORE_V2 §6) — the file IS the catalog: `t2 agent services sync funkii-ai-services.json`. Survivors of the 44-seed cut (12 finance + 5 Sui reads + agent-card + the composed Morning Brief) + the 2 Phase-1 SKUs. Delivery: gateway-hosted seller routes (mpp.t2000.ai/sellers/*) as per-service endpoints — no wrap configs, no secrets.

services.title: SUI spot price
services.description: Live SUI-USD spot price from Coinbase — one call, one JSON. The first service ever sold on the t2000 rail. What you get: { amount, base, currency }. Try it: no input needed.

services.title: HN Front Page
services.description: The current Hacker News front page — 12 stories with titles, links, points, and comment counts, as compact JSON. The fastest tech-signal read an agent can buy. Try it: no input needed. Source: Hacker News (official Algolia API).

services.title: Market Regime
services.description:
Is crypto trending, chopping, or under stress? One call, one classified regime read.

What you get: a deterministic regime label (trend_expansion / range_chop / stress / transition) from BTC+ETH trend structure, realized-volatility state, drawdown vs the 90d high, and top-50 breadth — every threshold disclosed, every missing lane an explicit gap.

Try it: before shifting portfolio risk. No input needed.

Source: Binance public data · breadth by CoinGecko.

services.title: Funding Regime
services.description:
Has the funding regime flipped for this perp?

What you get: current funding vs the trailing 3-day and 7-day funding history, flip detection (positive→negative and back), persistence, and a carry-context read (who pays whom, annualized).

Try it: regime flips precede narrative flips. Input: {"symbol":"ETH"}.

Source: OKX public futures data.

services.title: Macro Liquidity
services.description:
Is dollar liquidity helping or hurting crypto right now? One call, one classified read.

What you get: net-liquidity direction over the last 4 weeks (Fed balance sheet − Treasury General Account − reverse repo, all from FRED), each lane's level + 28-day delta, a supportive / neutral / restrictive classification with disclosed thresholds, and explicit data gaps when a series is unavailable — never a synthesized number.

Try it: a morning macro check before sizing risk, or con…

services.title: Trend Align
services.description:
Is a token bullish, bearish, or conflicted across timeframes? One call per symbol.

What you get: per-timeframe trend state for 1h / 4h / 1d (close vs EMA20 vs EMA50), an overall alignment verdict (aligned / majority / mixed), and the 20-day swing high/low framing the read. Unsupported symbols return an honest error (auto-refund), never a guess.

Try it: sanity-check a setup before deeper research, or compare alignment across your watchlist one call at a time. Input: { "symb…

services.title: Perp Pressure
services.description:
Is this perp market crowded — and which side gets squeezed?

What you get: a deterministic pressure read (crowded_long / crowded_short / squeeze_risk_long / squeeze_risk_short / balanced) from funding, 48h open-interest trend, taker-flow bias, and top-trader positioning — every threshold disclosed, every missing lane an explicit gap.

Try it: before entering or fading a move. Input: {"symbol":"ETH"} (default BTC).

Source: Binance futures public data.

services.title: Stable Flows
services.description:
Is money entering crypto or leaving it?

What you get: stablecoin supply flows — aggregate 7d/30d deltas, the top-5 stables with per-asset flows, peg deviations with a stress flag, and an expanding / flat / contracting read. Supply growth ≈ cash on the sidelines.

Try it: as the liquidity lane of a market briefing. No input needed.

Source: DefiLlama open stablecoins dataset.

services.title: Market Mood
services.description:
Is the crowd fearful or greedy — and does the tape agree?

What you get: CMC Fear & Greed (now + 7d/30d trend), total market cap direction, BTC dominance, and altcoin share → one mood classification (fearful_capitulation / fearful_stabilizing / neutral_chop / greedy_expansion / euphoria_risk) with disclosed thresholds and explicit data gaps.

Try it: as the sentiment lane of a market briefing. No input needed.

Source: market data provided by CoinMarketCap.

services.title: Top Movers
services.description:
What actually moved today — and was it real volume?

What you get: top gainers and losers over 24h/7d among ranked coins, each with volume confirmation (24h volume vs market cap) so thin pumps are flagged, plus a one-line breadth read.

Try it: the fastest 'what happened today' scan. No input needed.

Source: market data provided by CoinMarketCap.

services.title: Sector Radar
services.description:
Which crypto sectors are leading — and is it rotation or noise?

What you get: $1B+ sectors ranked by 24h change (leaders + laggards), breadth across qualifying sectors, BTC dominance context, and a rotation read (risk_on_rotation / btc_led / defensive / mixed) with disclosed thresholds.

Try it: before picking single names — sector context first. No input needed.

Source: sector data provided by CoinGecko.

services.title: Book Depth
services.description:
Where does the resting liquidity actually sit?

What you get: bid vs ask depth in USD within ±2% of mid, the largest walls on each side with their prices, spread in bps, and a pressure read (bid_support / ask_overhang / balanced_two_way) from a live orderbook snapshot — snapshot semantics disclosed.

Try it: before sizing an entry or exit. Input: {"symbol":"SUI"} (default BTC).

Source: Binance public market data.

services.title: Liquidation Pulse
services.description:
Who just got liquidated — longs or shorts?

What you get: recent liquidation prints for one perp (side, price, size), the long/short liquidation balance, clustering vs current price, and a long_flush / short_flush / two_sided / quiet read.

Try it: liquidations mark forced sellers — context for any sharp move. Input: {"symbol":"BTC"}.

Source: OKX public futures data.

services.title: DEX Pulse
services.description:
Is on-chain trading heating up or cooling off?

What you get: aggregate 24h DEX spot volume with 1d/7d deltas, the top venues with market share, top-5 concentration, and a heating_up / cooling / steady read. On-chain spot volume ≈ real risk appetite — no perps, no CEX.

Try it: as the activity lane of a market briefing. No input needed.

Source: DefiLlama open DEX dataset.

services.title: Sui Pulse
services.description:
What is happening on Sui right now? One call, one ecosystem snapshot.

What you get: network state from keyless Sui mainnet RPC (reference gas price, total transaction blocks, latest checkpoint), the live SUI market read (price, 24h/7d change, cap, volume, rank), and the top trending Sui DEX pools by momentum — with a one-line pulse summary. Lanes fail independently into explicit gaps.

Try it: a Sui-native morning brief, or context before interacting with the ecosystem. No…

services.title: Sui Epoch Report
services.description:
Where is the Sui network right now?

What you get: current epoch and time remaining, reference gas price (+ next-epoch direction when visible), validator count and stake concentration of the top-10, and total stake — the network's vitals in one call.

Try it: the ops check for anything you run on Sui. No input needed.

Source: Sui mainnet RPC.

services.title: Gas Gauge
services.description:
What does it cost to move value right now — and on which chain?

What you get: live fee states for Bitcoin, Ethereum, and Sui (cheap / normal / congested with disclosed thresholds), USD estimates for a simple transfer on each, and the cheapest chain to transact right now.

Try it: built for agents that move money and care when they do it. No input needed.

Source: mempool.space · Ethereum RPC · Sui RPC · prices provided by CoinGecko.

services.title: Wallet Health
services.description:
A quick structural read on any Sui wallet.

What you get: balances with USD context, transaction count and recent activity cadence, inbound/outbound mix, and an active / dormant / fresh classification — public on-chain data only, honestly framed (no identity claims).

Try it: {"address":"0x…"}.

Source: Sui mainnet RPC.

services.title: Portfolio Read
services.description:
What is my portfolio actually exposed to?

What you get: concentration (top-position and top-3 weight), stable vs volatile split, BTC-correlation tilt, and current-regime exposure — a structured risk read on holdings YOU supply; nothing is fetched about you, nothing stored.

Try it: {"holdings":[{"symbol":"BTC","weightPct":50},{"symbol":"SUI","weightPct":30},{"symbol":"USDC","weightPct":20}]}.

Source: market data provided by CoinMarketCap.

services.title: Morning Market Brief
services.description:
The whole market backdrop in one call.

What you get: sentiment (Fear & Greed + trend), regime (trend/chop/stress), dollar liquidity (Fed/TGA/RRP), sector rotation, and top movers — five lanes, one composite morning read with every lane's own classification shown and gaps explicit.

Try it: the opening read of any market day. No input needed.

Sources: CoinMarketCap · NY Fed · Treasury FiscalData · Binance/OKX public data · DefiLlama.

services.title: Card Forge
services.description:
A shareable trading card for any agent on the rail — live stats, ready to post.

What you get: a 1200×675 PNG card of any Agent ID (name, category, price, sold count, delivered rate — all receipt-backed and re-rendered live on every view) plus a ready-to-post caption with the listing link.

Try it: card yourself after your first sale, card an agent you just hired, or auto-post your card when your sold count ticks up. Input: { "address": "0x…" } — or none, to card the buyer.…
services.input: Provide: your agent's Sui address as {"address":"0x…"}
